_meta.note: LOCAL batch — mixed-archetype 5-pack across VA + GA + FL: rick-west (Mayor of Chesapeake VA, R, moderate-R civic mayor: 2A Constitutional City YES + tax-cut YES + pro-LE YES + anti-sanctuary YES vs first-ever-Pride-proclamation 2024 + COVID-vaccine PSA + mental-health framing Walmart shooting); andre-dickens (Mayor of Atlanta, D, STATE CORRECTION FL→GA, archetype-5 pro-police-progressive: Cop City magistrate-bears-sword + APD growth + Fani Willis lockstep + Big Bethel AME public Christian profession + charter-friendly board endorsements TRUE-cluster vs Pride-crosswalk + trans-funding + sickened-by-Dobbs + sanctuary + anti-2A FALSE-cluster); tim-bobanic (Brevard SOE FL, R, evidence_local-narrow: Clear Ballot 100% audit + signature-match + Burns mandamus institutional-discipline = EI-q0/q1 + PJ-q1 TRUE-only-3-cells with all other rubric cells NULL per admin-only constitutional-officer protocol — third FL Brevard constitutional-officer pattern alongside roster_verified [Blickley/Cullen] and evidence-local-with-audit-posture [Sadoff]); wayne-ivey (Brevard Sheriff FL, R, archetype-5 'Constitutional Sheriff' 14-TRUE/1-FALSE/15-NULL: pro-2A maximalist + anti-mandate + anti-sanctuary + Trump-endorser + In-God-We-Trust decals + Trinity Wellsprings Men's Ministry vs single Edwards-jail-death PJ-q4 accountability FALSE); gregory-tony (Broward Sheriff FL, D, archetype-5 progressive-D sheriff DeSantis-appointment-paradox: kneeled-with-BLM + $1M-implicit-bias + 'I-didn't-sign-up-to-be-ICE' + anti-permitless-carry + pro-RPO + ethics-censure + Jewish-menorah-flame-ban FALSE-cluster with 19 NULL on rubric cells outside sheriff lane). DICKENS STATE CORRECTION: set state=GA (currently FL — Atlanta is in Georgia, repo data error). BOBANIC PATTERN: establishes Brevard SOE-with-EI-record earns evidence_local on EI q0/q1 + PJ q1 specifically. IVEY EVANGELICAL FRAMING but no specific denomination — Christian-evangelical proxy via Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry speaking (March 2020) + In God We Trust patrol car decals defense (Fox & Friends Nov 2019) + DOJ pushback over school-board parents memo. TONY POLARITY CORRECTIONS: research agent flagged SD-q1 TRUE for 'supported red-flag/RPO' but Christian-conservative rubric scores pro-red-flag as FALSE on self-defense (consistent with Altman precedent where SB 7026 red-flag YES = FALSE); also flagged RSO-q1 TRUE for sanctuary-aligned local-vs-state autonomy but that is not what RSO measures on this rubric (already correctly captured as BI-q2 FALSE) — RSO-q1 corrected to NULL.

profile.background: Richard Wayne 'Rick' West (R-endorsed in nominally-nonpartisan office) — Mayor of Chesapeake, Virginia since November 14, 2017; re-elected November 5, 2024 with 55.7% over Don Carey III. Previously served ~10 years on Chesapeake City Council. Chesapeake Public Schools administrator for 20+ years before politics. Religion not findable in public bio/coverage.

rick-west.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: moderate-R 'broad-tent civic' mayor — explicit 'downplay the partisan thing' tone in 2024 campaign coverage. THREE TRUE-cluster cells: (1) self_defense q0 — led December 10 2019 unanimous 2A 'Constitutional City' resolution with on-record statement 'we will not just tell you to go to Richmond, we will go to Richmond with you'; (2) economic_stewardship q2 — moved a 4.5-cent real estate tax-rate cut in 2023 (failed 4-5 on Council) and Chesapeake adopted 4-cent rate cut in FY24 budget (from $1.05 to $1.01) which held flat in FY25 — documented tax-restraint posture; (3) public_justice q0 — affirmative pro-public-safety prioritization including Council eliminating curbside recycling specifically to fund public-safety pay raises with West in chair. SOFT TRUE: border_immigration q2 — Chesapeake actively partners with ICE under sheriff and Council took up resolution prohibiting sanctuary status (West has not contradicted institutional posture). THREE FALSE deviations: (1) biblical_marriage q4 — May 2024 issued FIRST-EVER Pride Month proclamation in Chesapeake history under city seal, citing 'immeasurable impact' of LGBTQIA+ residents and the Stonewall uprising (13NewsNow + Yahoo Lifestyle); (2) public_justice q3 — post-Walmart-shooting framing emphasized 'mental health needs or gun control or anything associated with the cause' rather than moral-evil framing, offered DHS mental healthcare; (3) refuse_state_overreach q1 — appeared in Feb 2021 City of Chesapeake PSA video promoting COVID-19 vaccine uptake and remained COVID-compliant throughout pandemic with no documented resistance to state directives. Sanctity-of-life cells are honest NULL (no abortion record locatable). Religion NULL — not findable in campaign bio (rickwestformayor.com/about), Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, or local press despite honest searching.

0.note: No personhood-from-conception advocacy located after searching Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, rickwestformayor.com campaign bio, 13NewsNow + WAVY + WTKR archives.

1.note: Mayor of city, not legislator; no abortion-restriction proclamations or Council actions located in Chesapeake records.

0.note: No public 1-man-1-woman affirmation located.

2.note: Chesapeake School Board (separate elected body, not the mayor) adopted Youngkin model trans policy 2023; not attributable to West. No mayoral SOGI/trans Council action located.

4.note: May 2024 — West issued FIRST-EVER Pride Month proclamation in Chesapeake history under city seal: 'LGBTQIA+ individuals have had immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community'; cited 1969 Stonewall uprising. HARD-FALSE on this cell — affirmatively endorsed Pride from the mayoral pulpit.

0.note: No mayoral school-choice/voucher advocacy located; school funding via separate elected school board.

1.note: No mayoral parental-notification action located.

2.note: No mayoral CRT/SOGI-curriculum statement located.

0.note: No public profession of Christian faith located in campaign bio (rickwestformayor.com/about), Wikipedia, or news coverage after honest searching.

2.note: No pronoun-mandate / compelled-speech action located.

3.note: Chesapeake Council adopted nonsectarian-prayer policy after 2009 ACLU pressure (predates West as mayor); no evidence West has championed Christian public-square displays since 2017.

4.note: No state-funded non-Christian display vote located.

2.note: Supported 4.5-cent real estate tax rate cut motion in 2023 (failed 4-5); FY24 budget adopted 4-cent rate cut from $1.05 to $1.01; FY25 held rate flat at $1.01. Documented tax-restraint posture.

0.note: No hand-counted-paper-ballot advocacy located.

1.note: VA voter-ID set statewide; no West-specific statement located.

4.note: No statement on private election grants located.

0.note: Affirmatively championed public-safety priority — Council eliminated curbside recycling specifically to fund public-safety pay raises with West in chair; Walmart-aftermath + Holly Cove-shooting statements credited first responders prominently.

1.note: No specific anti-soft-on-crime resolution located.

2.note: No proportional-restitution advocacy located.

3.note: Post-Walmart-shooting framing emphasized 'mental health needs or gun control or anything associated with the cause' and offered DHS mental healthcare — public-health/mental-health frame rather than moral-evil frame.

4.note: No magistrate-bears-sword framing located.

0.note: No documented local-control-vs-state-mandate resistance located.

1.note: Feb 2021 — West appeared in City of Chesapeake PSA video promoting COVID-19 vaccine; no documented resistance to state COVID directives. FALSE on COVID-resistance proxy.

2.note: No ESG-resistance action located.

4.note: No refusal-to-enforce state directive located.

1.note: Mayor lacks deportation authority; no specific advocacy located.

2.note: Chesapeake actively partners with ICE via jail/detainer process under Sheriff; Council previously took up resolution explicitly prohibiting sanctuary-city status. West has not contradicted this institutional posture under his mayoralty. Soft TRUE on institutional, not personal, basis.

0.note: Dec 10 2019 unanimous Chesapeake 'Constitutional City' 2A resolution; West stated on-record 'we will not just tell you to go to Richmond, we will go to Richmond with you' and 'your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia.' HARD-TRUE.

1.note: Mixed signal: led 2019 2A resolution (pro-2A baseline), but in 1-year Walmart anniversary referenced 'gun control' among causes discussed. Not a clear oppose-gun-control statement; not a clear pro-gun-control statement. NULL on honest reading.

profile.religion: African Methodist Episcopal (Big Bethel AME Church, Atlanta — public worship participation 2025)
profile.background: Andre Dickens (D) — 61st Mayor of Atlanta, GA; sworn January 3 2022 after defeating Felicia Moore in 2021 runoff; re-elected November 4 2025 with ~80% over Domagalski/Jackson/Meredith. Atlanta City Council Post 3 At-Large 2014-2021 (chair: Public Safety then Transportation). Georgia Tech chemical engineer, furniture-business owner, TechBridge nonprofit COO. STATE CORRECTION: repo previously had state=FL — Atlanta is in GA; corrected via set block.

andre-dickens.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: moderate-Black-Democrat-mayor pro-policing institutionalist — Bottoms-to-Dickens succession but MORE aggressively pro-APD than predecessor. SIGNATURE DEVIATION FROM D ARCHETYPE: spent enormous political capital to build Atlanta Public Safety Training Center ('Cop City') over sustained national protest movement; opened April 2025; defended as 'police reform' (de-escalation/crisis-intervention training); grew APD sworn ranks from ~1,600 to ~1,810 by 2026; retention bonuses + take-home cars; Fani Willis endorsed him citing 'lockstep' crime plan; cited 44% homicide drop. HARD-TRUE on public_justice q0 + q1 + q4 — strongest pro-LE record of any major-city D mayor in this scorecard. PUBLIC CHRISTIAN PROFESSION CL-q0 TRUE: delivered remarks at Big Bethel AME Annual Lay Day service April 27 2025, led the AME Call to Worship, joined hymn singing — reported by The Christian Recorder (AME's official publication). MODEST TRUE FCS-q0: endorsed APS school-board candidate Tony Mitchell backed by Equity in Education + GeorgiaCAN + City Fund Action charter-friendly slate. FALSE CLUSTER (10 cells): SOL q0+q1 — 'sickened by this decision that wrongly and immorally tells women that their bodies are not their own' post-Dobbs + backed Council resolution making enforcement of GA anti-abortion laws 'low priority' for APD + administration runs 'Reproductive Health & Wellness: Rights & Resources' portal; BM q2+q4 — unveiled Progress-Pride/trans-inclusive crosswalk at 10th & Piedmont + allocated $55K then $105K for trans-community orgs + explored gender-affirming care benefits for city-employee dependents in response to GA SB 140 + appointed trans advocate Dominique Morgan to Human Relations Commission + hosts 7th annual Mayor's Pride Reception at City Hall; EC q2 — publicly stated millage hike 'will happen' within 4 years + proposed FY24 $790M largest in city history + FY26 $3.0B operating budget; PJ q3 — One Atlanta Justice Reform frames low-level offenses through 'mental illness, homelessness, substance abuse' lens; BI q1+q2 — 'hoped ICE presence at World Cup would be small or nonexistent' + Atlanta on Center for Immigration Studies non-cooperation list + Council resolution opposing large-scale detention center inside city limits; SD q0+q1 — affirmatively called for mandatory safe-storage + universal background checks + red-flag + assault-weapons ban + criticized GA constitutional carry. STATE FIELD CORRECTION applied via set — Atlanta is in Georgia, not Florida (data/states/fl.json + candidates/fl/andre-dickens.html will need separate cleanup outside this refinement pipeline).

0.note: Post-Dobbs statement: 'sickened by this decision that wrongly and immorally tells women that their bodies are not their own'; explicit anti-personhood posture.

1.note: Backed Council resolution making enforcement of Georgia's anti-abortion laws a 'low priority' for APD; administration runs 'Reproductive Health & Wellness: Rights & Resources' portal directing residents to abortion access.

0.note: No affirmative 1-man-1-woman public statement located after searching campaign site, FB, X, press. AME affiliation alone does not satisfy rubric profession-of-marriage standard.

2.note: Unveiled Progress-Pride/trans-inclusive crosswalk at 10th & Piedmont; allocated $55K → $105K for trans-community organizations; explored gender-affirming care benefits for city-employee dependents in response to GA SB 140; appointed trans advocate Dominique Morgan to Human Relations Commission.

4.note: Hosts annual Mayor's Pride Reception at City Hall (7th annual in 2025); maintains Mayor's Pride Exhibit and Mayor's Division of LGBTQ+ Affairs; issued Pride Month proclamations.

0.note: Endorsed APS school-board candidate Tony Mitchell (Gilead lobbyist) backed by Equity in Education + GeorgiaCAN + City Fund Action — charter-friendly slate. Modest TRUE on alignment with charter-friendly board candidates; mayor has no direct voucher vote.

1.note: No direct mayoral vote or proclamation on parental-notification / minor-medical-consent located. Trans-youth funding tilts against parental-consent posture but is indirect; held to NULL on high-bar discipline.

2.note: No mayoral CRT/SOGI-curriculum statement on APS located; mayor has limited APS jurisdiction (separately elected board).

0.note: Delivered remarks at Big Bethel AME's Annual Lay Day service April 27 2025; led the AME Call to Worship; joined hymn singing ('Sweet Holy Spirit,' 'The Blood Still Works') — public Christian profession in a worship setting reported by the AME's official Christian Recorder.

2.note: No pronoun-mandate / compelled-speech vote or statement located.

3.note: No Sabbath / Christian public-square display advocacy located; AME attendance is private worship, not public-square display.

4.note: No state-funded non-Christian display vote located.

2.note: Publicly stated millage-rate hike 'will happen' within four years; proposed FY24 $790M general fund (largest in city history at the time); FY26 $3.0B operating budget. Has held millage rate flat to date but explicitly telegraphs future hike; no fiscal-watchdog posture.

0.note: No hand-counted-paper-ballot advocacy located; Atlanta uses Fulton County Dominion BMDs.

1.note: No direct Dickens statement on voter ID located. SB 202 era — D archetype implies opposition but no direct citation found. NULL on evidence-first discipline.

4.note: No statement re: Zuckerbucks/CTCL grants posture located.

0.note: HEADLINE DICKENS FACT — staked political career on building Cop City (opened April 2025); grew APD from ~1,600 to ~1,810 sworn; retention bonuses + take-home cars; Fani Willis endorsed him citing 'lockstep' crime plan. Strongest pro-LE record of any major-city D mayor in the scorecard.

1.note: One Safe City plan targets 'repeat offenders,' gang leaders, illegal guns; cited 44% homicide reduction. Mixed against cash-bond elimination for city-ordinance arrests but net anti-soft-on-crime posture on violent/repeat offenders.

2.note: No evidence on proportional restitution doctrine.

3.note: One Atlanta Justice Reform framework explicitly frames low-level offenses through 'mental illness, homelessness, and substance abuse' lens — public-health framing of crime root causes.

4.note: Cop City fight is the magistrate-bears-sword exemplar — stood up the training center over sustained protest, RICO indictments against organizers, contested referendum, 11th Circuit appeal — mayor used full institutional authority to enforce law-and-order infrastructure decision.

0.note: No clear local-control-vs-state-mandate vote located.

1.note: No documented direction-of-defense for local liberty against state mandate; trans-care exploration was directional-D, not RSO-TRUE.

2.note: No ESG-resistance action located.

4.note: No refusal-to-enforce state directive located.

1.note: Publicly stated he hoped ICE presence at World Cup would be 'small or nonexistent'; statement on federal presence at Hartsfield-Jackson framed as security-only, not enforcement. No mandatory-deportation advocacy.

2.note: Center for Immigration Studies lists Atlanta as non-cooperating with ICE; Atlanta City Council passed resolution opposing large-scale detention center inside city limits; mayor has not pushed reversal.

0.note: Publicly criticized Georgia's constitutional carry posture and called for restrictions; not a 2A defender.

1.note: Affirmatively called for mandatory safe-storage, universal background checks, red-flag laws, and a ban on assault weapons and high-capacity magazines.

profile.background: Tim Bobanic (R) — Brevard County Supervisor of Elections since October 1 2022 (appointed by Governor Ron DeSantis after Lori Scott's retirement). Previously ~10 years Brevard's Director of IT and Election Services under Scott; 4 years prior as IT Director for Hillsborough County SOE. BS Management Information Systems (USF); Master Florida Certified Elections Professional (MFCEP, 2020). Won 2024 R primary 59.62%-40.38% over outgoing County Commissioner John Tobia; advanced unopposed after write-in Sandra Cottrell withdrew. Religion not findable in public bio.

tim-bobanic.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: admin-only constitutional officer WITH measurable election-integrity record — third FL Brevard constitutional-officer pattern alongside (a) pure roster_verified [Blickley Property Appraiser + Cullen Tax Collector] and (b) evidence-local-with-fiscal-watchdog-audit [Sadoff Clerk/Comptroller]. Unlike Blickley/Cullen pure-admin, Bobanic earns evidence_local because his statutory wheelhouse IS election integrity — producing measurable output on the rubric's election-integrity axis. THREE TRUE cells: (1) election_integrity q0 — implemented and operates Brevard's 100% Clear Ballot independent post-election audit covering every race + precinct + ballot with 99.992% certified-to-audit match in 2024 General; all FL votes on paper, 22-month retention per statute; (2) election_integrity q1 — office enforces FL photo-ID-with-signature requirement at Early Voting + Election Day, mail ballots require name + DOB + addresses + DL or last-4-SSN + signature match; implemented SigView signature-verification system, won 2023 EAC Clearinghouse Award; (3) public_justice q1 — when Burns v. Bobanic mandamus alleged unequal treatment of R vs D candidate qualifying paperwork, Bobanic responded with internal audit + fired responsible staffer + publicly noted 3 of 4 disqualified candidates that cycle were Republicans (suggests non-partisan administration); Circuit Judge Scott Blaue dismissed suit for lack of standing. ALL OTHER RUBRIC CELLS NULL per LOCAL skill admin-only-constitutional-officer protocol — no findable statements on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family/child sovereignty, christian_liberty, border/immigration, self-defense, or broader fiscal positions outside statutory office duties. Religion not findable. EI q4 NULL — FL statutorily banned private election grants in SB 90 (2021); Brevard operates under ban but no Bobanic-specific public Zuckerbucks/CTCL statement located.

0.note: Implemented and operates Brevard's 100% Clear Ballot independent post-election audit covering every race, every precinct, every ballot, with 99.992% certified-to-audit match in 2024 General. All FL votes cast on paper ballots stored 22 months per statute.

1.note: Office enforces FL photo-ID-with-signature requirement at Early Voting and Election Day; mail ballots require name + DOB + addresses + FL DL or last-4 SSN plus signature match. Implemented SigView signature-verification system, 2023 U.S. EAC Clearinghouse Award.

4.note: FL statutorily banned private election grants in SB 90 (2021); Brevard operates under ban, but no Bobanic-specific public Zuckerbucks/CTCL statement located after searching FL Politics, Space Coast Daily, Space Coast Rocket, Florida Today, and direct Zuckerbucks/CTCL search.

1.note: Burns v. Bobanic mandamus suit alleged unequal treatment of R vs D candidate qualifying paperwork. Bobanic responded with internal audit, fired the responsible staffer, and publicly noted 3 of 4 disqualified candidates that cycle were Republicans (suggests non-partisan administration). Circuit Judge Scott Blaue dismissed suit for lack of standing.

profile.religion: Christian / Evangelical (proxy: Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry Breakfast speaker March 2020; no specific denomination publicly confirmed)
profile.background: Wayne Ivey (R) — Sheriff of Brevard County, Florida since January 2013; unopposed 2016 + 2020; won 4th term November 2024 with ~87% as sole candidate. Nationally known for weekly 'Wheel of Fugitive' Facebook videos and 'Constitutional Sheriff' identity (rebranded FB page 'Constitutional Sheriff Wayne Ivey American Patriot'). Endorsed by Gov. Ron DeSantis (Aug 2024) and early Trump endorser (Jan 2024).

wayne-ivey.notes_set: HIGH-DEVIATION-RICH archetype-5 'Constitutional Sheriff' profile — Trump-aligned + 2A maximalist + anti-sanctuary + anti-mandate + openly Christian framing. 14 TRUE cells across PJ/RSO/BI/SD/CL/FCS clusters. HEADLINE TRUE CLUSTER: (1) RSO 5/5 TRUE — 2013 signed statement refusing federal gun-control enforcement, 'we are not the mask police' Oct 2021 deputies-will-not-enforce-school-masks, 'stands with America's workforce in fight against vaccine mandates' Oct 2021, rebuked DOJ for 'threats of violence' framing of school-board-protest parents, 'Constitutional Sheriff' public identity; (2) BI 2/2 TRUE — 'You bring that crap to Brevard County, and you're going to end up in jail' anti-sanctuary, Brevard launched 287(g) in 2019 (pre-DeSantis statewide), Feb 2025 joined DeSantis Illegal Immigration Street Task Force, BCSO awarded ~$700K immigration-enforcement funding Feb 2026; (3) SD 2/2 TRUE — viral 'arm up and prepare for an attack' video calling on citizens to arm themselves, attended April 2023 constitutional carry signing 'Criminals don't go get a permit,' Sept 2025 open-carry message announcing deputies will not enforce ban after 1st DCA ruling; (4) PJ 4/5 TRUE — Wheel of Fugitive + chain-gang program (modeled on Arpaio 2013) + 'encouraging citizens to arm themselves and confront bad guys before deputies arrive' (Marshall Project), 'zero tolerance for crime keeps FL safe' flvoicenews, June 2025 anti-riot 'we will kill you, graveyard dead' press conference with AG Uthmeier, right-wing-law-enforcement-ideology alignment; (5) CL 3/4 TRUE — Defended 'In God We Trust' patrol-car decals on Fox & Friends Nov 2019, rebuked DOJ school-board-parents-as-threats memo, spoke at Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry Breakfast March 2020 'about his faith and protecting the foundation of the nation'; (6) FCS q0 TRUE — 'We are not the mask police' framed around parents' authority over their children. SINGLE FALSE DEVIATION: PJ q4 — Gregory Edwards 2018 in-custody death (Army combat vet, restraint chair, pepper spray, spit hood, stun-gun barbs in back); BCSO fought 2+ years to release jail video, FLORIDA TODAY had to sue under Public Records Act (released Nov 2020); federal §1983 force claims dismissed Mar 2023 but state negligence claim survived — pattern of accountability resistance, not reform. SOL all NULL — sheriff lane discipline; no abortion statement located; do NOT infer from R-party or evangelical framing. BM all NULL — no specific marriage/SOGI/Pride statement located. EC q2 NULL — no documented fiscal-restraint posture in agency. CAREFUL DATA HYGIENE: research agent honestly flagged that prior batch-prompt confused (a) Marion County's Susan Lorincz/Santiago-Miranda case (Sheriff Billy Woods, NOT Brevard), (b) GA Laken Riley case (Jose Ibarra) with the Brevard Aug 2021 Paris Wilder traffic-stop ambush (deputy Brian Potters wounded NOT killed) — neither belongs to Ivey's record; not scored.

0.note: No abortion/pro-life statement located. Sheriff lane discipline — NOT inferring from R-party affiliation or evangelical framing.

1.note: No FL Amendment 4 (6-week ballot question Nov 2024) statement located.

0.note: No statement located.

2.note: No Ivey-specific SOGI/drag/groomers statement located despite alignment with post-2022 conservative Brevard School Board majority.

4.note: No Pride-flag refusal statement located.

0.note: Oct 2021 — 'We are not the mask police'; deputies will not enforce school mask mandates, framed around parents' authority over their children; rebuked DOJ for investigating parents who voiced opposition to mask mandates.

1.note: No Ivey-specific gender-transition-for-minors statement located.

2.note: Outside sheriff lane; no school-choice/govt-school-monopoly statement located.

0.note: Defended 'In God We Trust' decals on BCSO patrol cars on Fox & Friends Nov 2019 against Freedom From Religion Foundation objections — public Christian-public-square defense.

2.note: No church-closure-specific COVID statement located.

3.note: Oct 2021 — Ivey publicly opposed DOJ effort to investigate parents as 'threats of violence' over school-board mask debate, calling the framing inappropriate.

4.note: March 2020 — spoke at Trinity Wellsprings Church (Satellite Beach) Men's Ministry Breakfast 'about his faith and protecting the foundation of the nation' — open Christian public profession as sheriff.

2.note: No documented fiscal-restraint posture in agency located.

0.note: Nationally famous for 'Wheel of Fugitive,' chain-gang program modeled on Joe Arpaio (2013), and 'encouraging citizens to arm themselves and confront bad guys before deputies arrive.' Viral 'arm up & prepare for an attack' video. Magistrate-bears-sword posture.

1.note: Article framed around Ivey's explicit 'zero tolerance for crime' posture as central to Brevard safety record.

2.note: June 2025 press conference with AG Uthmeier ahead of 'No Kings' protests: 'If you throw a brick, a firebomb, or point a gun at one of our deputies, we will be notifying your family where to collect your remains at. Because we will kill you, graveyard dead.' Hard-line anti-riot.

3.note: Public alignment with right-wing law-enforcement ideology, 'Constitutional Sheriff' rebrand, consistent opposition to defund movement implicit in 2020 race messaging.

4.note: DEVIATION: Gregory Edwards 2018 in-custody death (Army combat vet, restraint chair, pepper spray, spit hood, stun-gun barbs in back); BCSO fought 2+ years to release jail video, FLORIDA TODAY had to sue under PRA (released Nov 2020); federal §1983 force claims dismissed Mar 2023 but state negligence claim survived. Pattern of accountability resistance, not reform — q4 FALSE on high-bar discipline.

0.note: 2013 signed statement refusing to enforce federal gun-control actions; rebranded FB page 'Constitutional Sheriff Wayne Ivey American Patriot.'

1.note: 'We are not the mask police' — deputies will not enforce school mask mandates.

2.note: Oct 2021 — Ivey publicly 'stands with America's workforce in fight against vaccine mandates.'

4.note: 2013 signed statement refusing to enforce federal gun control; 'Constitutional Sheriff' public identity; called out 'anti-Second Amendment extremists' Ammoland April 2021.

1.note: 'You bring that crap to Brevard County, and you're going to end up in jail.' Anti-sanctuary, pro-enforcement signature.

2.note: Brevard launched 287(g) in 2019 (years before DeSantis statewide expansion); Ivey joined Feb 2025 Illegal Immigration Street Task Force announcement; BCSO awarded ~$700K for immigration enforcement work.

1.note: Attended FL HB 543 constitutional carry signing April 2023: 'This bill is an important piece of legislation for our citizens to have the ability to protect themselves. Criminals don't go get a permit.' Sept 2025 open-carry video announcing deputies will not enforce ban after 1st DCA ruling.

profile.background: Gregory Scott Tony (D, b. 1978 Philadelphia) — 17th Sheriff of Broward County, first Black sheriff in county history. Appointed by Gov. Ron DeSantis January 11 2019 after DeSantis suspended Scott Israel over Parkland response. Former Coral Springs PD sergeant (2005-2016), FSU football player, co-founded Blue Spear Solutions active-shooter training firm. Won 2020 D primary narrowly over Israel, re-elected November 5 2024 with 66.57% (531,594 votes) defeating independent Charles Whatley. Religion not publicly disclosed; has engaged both interfaith/Christian clergy (BOLD Justice coalition) and Islamic Center of South Florida outreach.

gregory-tony.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: progressive-D sheriff DeSantis-appointment-paradox — appointed by DeSantis but ideologically left, walks a tightrope. HEAVY FALSE CLUSTER (9 cells): (1) PJ q0 — kneeled with BLM protesters June 2020 outside BSO HQ, 'I have personally been the guy on the ground, unjustly, with the knee on his neck'; signed Broward chiefs' apology for 'systemic racism'; $1M racial-equity/implicit-bias training; 'I fired the bad cops' campaign-ad theme; (2) PJ q2 — pushed adult civil-citation diversion program for nonviolent offenders 2019-2023; (3) PJ q3 — ethics-record honesty FALSE: 1993 killing of Hector 'Chino' Rodriguez at age 14 undisclosed on multiple LE applications, prior LSD use and PA license suspension also undisclosed; CJSTC sustained perjury finding Feb 2025; ethics censure + DeSantis reprimand March 2025; April 2026 finding he used confidential police data to obtain PhD; (4) PJ q4 — sanctuary-aligned, 'didn't sign up to be ICE'; (5) BI q1 — explicit refusal posture pre-Uthmeier pressure 'refuses to work with ICE agents who arrest immigrants who are not criminals'; (6) BI q2 — only ~2 BSO staff assigned to 287(g); explicit 'not a priority' language; backed down rhetorically June 10 2025 under AG Uthmeier pressure but functional posture remained minimal-cooperation; (7) SD q0 — actively lobbied Broward delegation Jan 2023 against permitless concealed carry, '100,000 plus 10%' opposed; (8) SD q1 — publicly touts FL Risk Protection Orders (red-flag), BSO submitted 400 RPOs with 400 approvals removing ~750 firearms — pro-red-flag is FALSE on this rubric (consistent with Altman precedent where SB 7026 red-flag YES scored FALSE on SD q1); (9) EC q2 — Ethics Commission probable-cause for misuse of position + April 2026 confidential-data finding undermines fiscal-stewardship; (10) CL q2 — Dec 2022 BSO prohibited Jewish inmates from lighting real-flame menorah during Hanukkah, Rep. Wasserman Schultz + rabbis called out 'pattern of insensitivity' — religious-accommodation deviation FALSE. 19 NULL cells: sheriff role has limited footprint on abortion, marriage, family-sovereignty, election-integrity, RSO, christian-liberty rubric cells. Note: research agent flagged RSO-q1 TRUE for asserting county autonomy vs state immigration mandate but on Christian-conservative rubric defending sanctuary is NOT a RSO TRUE — corrected to NULL (the substantive content already correctly scored as BI-q2 FALSE). 2024 opponent was Charles Whatley (IND), NOT Allen Foley as research-prompt erroneously suggested. Religion unknown.

0.note: No abortion/pro-life statements located in reviewed sources.

1.note: Sheriff role has no direct abortion-policy nexus; no relevant admin action located.

0.note: No marriage statements located.
